summary: Wallet/system credential stealer with hardcoded C2 and worm-style propagation
details: This package masquerades as a DeFi threat scanner but ships three files implementing a credential and wallet-stealer with self-propagation. scanner.js imports child_process, os, fs, and https, runs whoami/os.hostname() and execSync to enumerate the host, and POSTs results to a hardcoded C2 at https://ddjidd564.github.io (a GitHub Pages-hosted endpoint, lines 161 and 323). wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem enumeration (multiple fs.existsSync checks), executes shell commands (curl on lines 524, 758, 783, 868; ping on line 351), gathers host identity (os.hostname() at lines 617 and 694, execSync at line 693), and POSTs the harvested data to attacker infrastructure (lines 4, 448, 520, 596). worm-injector.js — the file name itself describes its purpose — repeatedly invokes child_process and POSTs to a hardcoded C2 (lines 312, 315, 629, 1452, 1462), consistent with self-propagation/auto-injection logic. Installing or loading this package exfiltrates host identity, filesystem state, and likely browser/wallet artifacts to attacker-controlled endpoints.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
